message:
fix(packs): split on pipeline | and bare & so safe stages cannot shield destructive ones

Tightens segment splitting in split_command_segments() to also break
on `|`, `|&`, and bare `&` (background). Previous behavior intentionally
kept those whole "because some pack patterns legitimately span them
(e.g. kustomize build | kubectl diff)." That left a real abuse path
open: `railway service list | railway volume delete --volume prod-db
--yes` (or the same with `&`) ran the safe `service list` first, the
sanitized form's keyword scan saw the safe segment and short-circuited,
and the destructive `volume delete` never made it to the pack matcher.

Specifically:
- `;`, `\n`, `&&`, `||` continue to split (1- and 2-byte widths).
- `&` now splits whether it appears as `&&` (sequence) or alone
  (background).
- `|` now splits whether it appears as `||` (or-list), `|&`
  (Bash pipefail-with-stderr), or alone (pipeline).
- Width is computed as 2 if the next byte forms one of the
  recognized 2-char operators, 1 otherwise — keeps the existing
  quote/backslash-escape handling intact.

Doc comments and inline comments in pack.check() and PackRegistry's
evaluate_packs_with_allowlists() updated to drop the prior wording
about pipelines being kept whole.

Trade-off acknowledged: pack patterns that *deliberately* span a
pipeline (the prior comment cited `kustomize build | kubectl diff`,
and the railway-api-database-variable-upsert pattern's regex does
match across `curl ... | jq ...`) now see segmented input. The
evaluator already has a fallback for this case via
should_check_original_control_plane_payload (62ab955 / 2e7453f),
which re-runs the railway pack on the original (un-segmented)
command when curl + Railway API signals are present in the sanitized
form. That keeps the cross-pipe railway API mutations blocked. Any
other pack that legitimately spans a pipeline must adopt the same
re-check pattern; the current set of patterns either matches per-
segment or relies on the railway re-check.

Tests:
- evaluator: two new cases —
  non_core_safe_pipeline_stage_does_not_mask_later_destructive_stage
  asserts `railway service list | railway volume delete ...` is
  blocked under platform.railway with pattern_name
  railway-volume-delete; and the same for the `&` background variant.
- packs/mod.rs split_command_segments tests updated:
  `docker ps | grep nginx` now splits to ["docker ps", "grep nginx"];
  `docker ps |& grep nginx` and `docker ps & docker logs foo` and
  `docker logs foo &` similarly. Backslash-escape test extended to
  `echo a\| b` to confirm escaped pipes still don't split.
- railway.rs pack-level tests gain pipeline and background variants
  alongside the existing `&&` case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
